evidenceRefs.title: Dota2 Host Realization Policy > Dota2 Capability Awareness
evidenceRefs.snippet:
The policy may assume the following high-level host facts:

- Dota2 has server/shared/UI output surfaces
- Dota2 supports host-native KV configuration for many ability properties
- Dota2 also requires script logic for more complex runtime behavior
- UI is realized through Panoram

evidenceRefs.title: Dota2 Host Realization Policy > Decision Order v1
evidenceRefs.snippet:
For each realization unit, Dota2 Host Realization v1 should apply roughly this order:

1. Is the unit clearly a UI surface?
- if yes, route to `ui`

2. Is the unit clearly expressible as host-native static configuration only?
- if yes, bias to `kv`

3. Does the unit require custo

evidenceRefs.title: Host Realization Contract > Decision Rules
evidenceRefs.snippet:
Host Realization must prefer rules over freeform model behavior.

For the current project stage, Host Realization v1 should be implemented as:

- host-specific
- rule-first
- deterministic
- reviewable

It should not initially depend on a separate realization LLM.

This does not

evidenceRefs.title: Dota2 Host Realization Policy > KV + TS Hybrid Cases
evidenceRefs.snippet:
The following categories should generally bias toward `kv+ts`:

- Dota2 ability shell defined statically, but behavior implemented dynamically
- modifier registration via host-native naming/config with runtime logic in TS
- features where the host expects ability or modifier meta

evidenceRefs.title: Dota2 Events Knowledge > 5. 事件分类速查 > 按用途分类
evidenceRefs.snippet:
```
lifecycle          -> 游戏生命周期（开始、结束、状态切换）
player             -> 玩家相关（连接、断开、操作）
combat             -> 战斗相关（击杀、伤害、治疗）
ability            -> 技能相关（施法、冷却、效果）
ui                 -> 界面相关（点击、悬停、动画）
```

| `GetCursorTarget()` | 获取目标单位 |
| `GetCursorPosition()` | 获取目标位置 |
| `GetSpecialValueFor(name)` | 获取 KV 中定义的数值 |

|------|------|
| `GetCooldown(level)` | 获取冷却时间

evidenceRefs.title: AbilityBehavior.DOTA_ABILITY_BEHAVIOR_AFFECTED_BY_MUTE
evidenceRefs.snippet: [engine] enum-member | sig: AFFECTED_BY_MUTE

assumptions: Q activation is satisfied by placing this active ability in the unit's Q slot through existing game data or bindings outside this artifact.
The fire orb can be represented host-natively by a particle attached to the caster rather than a separate controllable unit.
Nearby enemy damage is centered on the caster's current position because the orb follows the caster continuously.
If ability special values are not defined in KV, safe fallback values are used: duration 2.0, radius 250, damage 40, interval 0.5, height 120.
No UI, inventory, or persistence behavior is implemented, per feature constraints.
The ability will be bound to Q by normal ability-slot placement outside this KV artifact.
Orb follow behavior, periodic ticking, enemy queries, and expiration are implemented in the referenced Lua ScriptFile.
A basic 4-level scaling is acceptable because the blueprint did not specify level count or damage values.
Including DOTA_ABILITY_BEHAVIOR_AFFECTED_BY_MUTE is acceptable for an active castable ability unless gameplay design later requires otherwise.

reviewReasons: Module 'mod_typed_trigger_q_activate' was synthesized because no reusable family/pattern implementation fully matched it.
Module includes synthesized host-native implementation and should remain reviewable.

evidenceRefs.title: AbilityBehavior.DOTA_ABILITY_BEHAVIOR_AFFECTED_BY_MUTE
evidenceRefs.snippet: [engine] enum-member | sig: AFFECTED_BY_MUTE

assumptions: Q-triggered activation is represented by this standard active Lua ability's OnSpellStart entry point.
The fire orb instance is realized as a temporary feature-owned runtime state via a hidden modifier plus a particle emitter, rather than a persistent spawned world entity.

Because no KV file artifact was requested here, gameplay values use ability special values when present and otherwise fall back to code defaults: duration 2.0, damage 30, radius 250, damage interval 0.5, follow distance 110, follow height 96, vision radius 300.
Nearby enemy damage is centered on the orb's computed follow position in front of and above the caster, updating continuously while active.
Recasting while active refreshes the same orb state on the caster instead of creating multiple simultaneous orbs.
The declared ability name is rw_micro_feature_sewp_mod_typed_spawn_fire_orb, matching the provided deterministic fallback.
The server-side Lua script at rune_weaver/abilities/rw_micro_feature_sewp_mod_typed_spawn_fire_orb will implement orb spawning, follow behavior, timed expiration, and periodic enemy damage.
Duration, tick interval, radius, and damage are exposed in AbilitySpecial for host-native runtime configuration.
Using DOTA_ABILITY_BEHAVIOR_NO_TARGET is appropriate for a Q-triggered active skill with no target selection.
Including DOTA_ABILITY_BEHAVIOR_AFFECTED_BY_MUTE is acceptable because retrieved evidence referenced this behavior flag and it does not add UI surface.

reviewReasons: Module 'mod_typed_spawn_fire_orb' was synthesized because no reusable family/pattern implementation fully matched it.
Module includes synthesized host-native implementation and should remain reviewable.

evidenceRefs.title: AbilityBehavior.DOTA_ABILITY_BEHAVIOR_AFFECTED_BY_MUTE
evidenceRefs.snippet: [engine] enum-member | sig: AFFECTED_BY_MUTE

assumptions: The Q-triggered activation is satisfied by wiring this ability into the appropriate ability slot/key binding outside this artifact.
The orb-following behavior is represented as ephemeral server-side state on the caster via a modifier rather than a separate spawned controllable unit.
Ability special values may be provided in KV using orb_duration, interval_seconds, damage_radius, damage_per_tick, orb_offset_distance, and fow_radius; safe defaults are used when absent.
Nearby enemy lookup uses standard server-side radius queries centered on the computed orb position.
Periodic damage is applied on the server using ApplyDamage with the ability's configured damage type and target filters.
The Q key binding is handled by normal Dota 2 ability slot assignment outside this KV artifact.
The orb-following behavior and periodic damage logic are implemented in the referenced Lua ScriptFile.
A no-target active ability is the correct host-native activation surface for this feature.
Special values duration, interval_seconds, radius, and damage are sufficient for the paired server runtime implementation.

reviewReasons: Module 'mod_typed_orb_periodic_damage' was synthesized because no reusable family/pattern implementation fully matched it.
Module includes synthesized host-native implementation and should remain reviewable.

evidenceRefs.title: AbilityBehavior.DOTA_ABILITY_BEHAVIOR_AFFECTED_BY_MUTE
evidenceRefs.snippet: [engine] enum-member | sig: AFFECTED_BY_MUTE

assumptions: This artifact is the server-side Lua ability file only; ability KV wiring, hotkey/Q binding, and ScriptFile linkage are defined elsewhere in the owned config scope.

The orb instance is represented host-natively as a timed modifier plus attached particle/sound on the caster, which satisfies the temporary follow behavior without creating a separate persistent entity.
If SpecialValues are absent in KV, the implementation falls back to duration=2.0, tick_interval=0.5, radius=300, and damage_per_tick=30.
Nearby enemy damage is evaluated around the caster's current position because the orb follows the caster continuously.
Refreshing/recasting while active reapplies or refreshes the same modifier rather than spawning multiple concurrent orb instances.
The skill is implemented as a standard active no-target ability, which can be bound to Q through normal Dota ability slot assignment rather than explicit KV key-binding data.
A Lua script at rune_weaver/abilities/rw_micro_feature_sewp_mod_func_0 will handle orb spawning, following behavior, timed expiration, and periodic enemy damage.
AFFECTED_BY_MUTE is included so the active skill respects mute state.
Single-level tuning is sufficient for this micro feature.
Damage radius was set to 250 as a reasonable host-native default for 'nearby enemies'.
Damage per tick was set to 30 as a reasonable host-native default because the blueprint did not specify damage numbers.

reviewReasons: Module 'mod_func_0' was synthesized because no reusable family/pattern implementation fully matched it.
Module includes synthesized host-native implementation and should remain reviewable.

exports.summary: Temporary active fire orb following the caster.
Orb expiration after its timed duration.
Entry point for Q-triggered skill activation.
Host-side lookup for enemies near the orb or caster-following orb position.

consumes.summary: Activate the skill when Q is pressed.
Spawn a fire orb that follows the caster.
The fire orb periodically damages nearby enemies.

stateScopes.summary: Temporary active fire orb following the caster.
Orb expiration after its timed duration.
